title: Finite Lanczos Iteration Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite Lanczos bad tridiagonal coefficient QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact Lanczos replay computes beta1 = 1 from the first residual norm while a malformed row claims beta1 = 2, isolating a compact equality conflict for the Farkas route.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional Lanczos, symmetric Arnoldi, or CG rows only when they add distinct tridiagonalization, residual, restart, Ritz-value, or numerical-honesty pressure; keep convergence, finite-precision orthogonality, and floating-point stability in Lean or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed symmetric rational matrix, starting vector, two Lanczos basis vectors, alpha/beta coefficients, residual norm, tridiagonal matrix, and exact matrix relation as deterministic exact-rational replay obligations.
Route the false first off-diagonal tridiagonal coefficient through a source-linked QF_LRA artifact and checked Farkas evidence after exact replay computes beta1 = 1.
Expose the pack through matrix computation, residual-bound, eigenpair/operator, inner-product, and exact-vs-floating learner and consumer query surfaces.
Keep general Lanczos convergence, Ritz value theory, breakdown/restart policies, finite-precision loss of orthogonality, and floating-point Krylov stability marked Lean or numerical-honesty horizon.
